name: MCPBench — MCP Server Benchmarking Tool
what_it_does: MCPBench benchmarking and evaluation framework for MCP servers — testing MCP server correctness, measuring latency and throughput, evaluating tool response quality, and providing standardized benchmarks to compare MCP server implementations for agent-driven quality assurance and MCP development workflows.
use_cases: Benchmarking custom MCP server performance from MCP development agents
Testing MCP server correctness against specification from QA agents
Comparing latency and throughput across MCP implementations
Evaluating MCP server response quality and accuracy
Load testing MCP servers before production deployment from DevOps agents
Continuous integration testing of MCP server implementations
not_for: Running as an MCP server itself (it's a testing/benchmarking tool)
Production monitoring (use Prometheus/Datadog for production observability)
best_when: An MCP server developer needs to benchmark, test correctness, and measure performance of their MCP server implementation.
avoid_when: You're consuming MCP servers (not building them) — use the MCPs directly.

auth.notes: No authentication — testing tool that connects to target MCP servers. Target MCP server credentials configured separately.

performance.rate_limits: Local tool — performance constrained by target MCP server

known_agent_gotchas: Benchmark results depend on target MCP server configuration — not a general benchmarking tool
Community project — benchmark scenarios may not cover all MCP tool types
LLM-based evaluation requires API keys for AI judge models
Results may vary significantly based on hardware and network conditions
Not a protocol validator — correctness checks may be incomplete
Development tool, not intended for production monitoring pipelines
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local testing tool. No sensitive data stored. Target MCP server credentials handled separately. Community tool from mcp-ecosystem.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local testing tool. No credentials stored. Community tool.
